---
title: "Linked Open Exhibition"
subtitle: "Sprengel Museum Hannover — Prototype"
author: "BIM-126-02 Data Science: Simon Worthington; Prof. Ina Blümel"
date: today
---

## About this project

This site presents a prototype pipeline for publishing art exhibition data as **Linked Open Data (LOD)**. It was created with students of the Bachelor of Arts Information Management at Hochschule Hannover in the course *BIM-126-02 Data Science (SoSe 2026)*.

**Goal:** Retrieve exhibition catalogue records from the Deutsche Nationalbibliothek (DNB), structure them using an open data model, upload them to a Wikibase knowledge base, and publish the results as a Quarto website — all reproducibly and with full documentation.

## The pipeline

```
DNB SRU API → MARC21-XML → CSV → Wikibase → SPARQL → Quarto website
```

| Step | Notebook | Output |
|---|---|---|
| 1. Retrieve DNB records | `01_dnb_search.ipynb` | `sprengel_raw.xml` |
| 2. Filter & extract fields | `02_dnb_filter_exhibitions.ipynb` | `sprengel_exhibitions.csv` |
| 3. Fetch cover images | `03_dnb_cover_images.ipynb` | `images/*.jpg` |
| 4. Upload data model | `04_wikibase_data_model.ipynb` | Wikibase properties |
| 5. Upload exhibitions | `05_wikibase_upload.ipynb` | Wikibase items |
| 6. Upload images | `06_mediawiki_upload.ipynb` | MediaWiki files |

## Explore

- [Exhibitions →](exhibitions.qmd) — browse the Sprengel Museum exhibition timeline, powered by live SPARQL queries
- [Data Analysis →](data-analysis.qmd) — explore the full pipeline and dataset
- [About →](about.qmd) — licences, credits, and AI attribution

## Course & resources

- **Wikiversity course page:** [BIM-126-02-Data-Science-Linked-Open-Exhibition](https://en.wikiversity.org/wiki/BIM-126-02-Data-Science-Linked-Open-Exhibition)
- **Wikibase instance:** [wikibase.wbworkshop.tibwiki.io](https://wikibase.wbworkshop.tibwiki.io/)
- **Source repository:** [NFDI4Culture/linked-open-exhibition](https://github.com/NFDI4Culture/linked-open-exhibition)
- **Sprengel Museum Hannover:** [sprengel-museum.de](https://www.sprengel-museum.de/)

## Wikidata exhibition timeline

Live query against Wikidata for Sprengel Museum exhibitions entered by students in Sessions 1–3:

```{=html}
<iframe style="width: 100%; height: 500px; border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 4px;"
  src="https://query.wikidata.org/embed.html#%23title%3A%20Visual%20Timeline%20of%20exhibitions%20at%20the%20Sprengel%20Museum%0A%23defaultView%3ATimeline%0ASELECT%20%3Fexhibition%20%3FexhibitionLabel%20%3Fdate%20%3Fimage%20WHERE%20%7B%0A%20%20%3Fexhibition%20wdt%3AP31%2Fwdt%3AP279%2a%20wd%3AQ464980%3B%0A%20%20%20%20%20%20%20%20%20%20%20%20%20%20wdt%3AP276%20wd%3AQ510144.%0A%20%20OPTIONAL%20%7B%20%3Fexhibition%20wdt%3AP571%20%3Fdate.%20%7D%0A%20%20OPTIONAL%20%7B%20%3Fexhibition%20wdt%3AP580%20%3Fdate.%20%7D%0A%20%20OPTIONAL%20%7B%20%3Fexhibition%20wdt%3AP18%20%3Fimage.%20%7D%0A%20%20FILTER%28BOUND%28%3Fdate%29%29%0A%20%20SERVICE%20wikibase%3Alabel%20%7B%20bd%3AserviceParam%20wikibase%3Alanguage%20%22%5BAUTO_LANGUAGE%5D%2Cen%2Cde%22.%20%7D%0A%7D%0AORDER%20BY%20%3Fdate"
  referrerpolicy="origin"
  sandbox="allow-scripts allow-same-origin allow-popups">
</iframe>
```

::: aside
 
:::

## Sitzung 2: Die Übungen {.smaller}

- Ausstellungsaktualisierung
- Künstler
- Ausstellungskatalog
- AI LLM SPARQL-Experimente

## Sitzungsplan 

11:00-11:15 Einführung und Datenmodellierung <br />
11:15-11:45 Ausstellungsaktualisierung <br /> 
11:45-12:15 Künstler <br />
12:15-12:25 Pause <br />
12:25-13:05 Ausstellungskatalog <br />
13:05-13:50 AI LLM SPARQL-Experimente <br />
13:50-14:15 Abschließende Diskussion, Fragen und Antworten, Planung für die nächste Sitzung

## Hausaufgaben

- Erstellen Sie ein Datenmodell für ein Kunstwerk in einer Ausstellung.
- Was sind die Mindestangaben, die erforderlich sind, um ein Kunstwerk in einer Ausstellung zu präsentieren?
- [Link](https://en.wikiversity.org/wiki/BIM-126-02-Data-Science-Linked-Open-Exhibition#Hausaufgaben%C3%BCbungen)

## Danke 

Siehe [Linked Open Data für Kunstausstellungen](https://github.com/NFDI4Culture/linked-open-exhibition)

Simon Worthington - [simon.worthington@tib.eu](mailto:simon.worthington@tib.eu)
